The ancient Egyptians referred to their land as Kemet, which translates to "black land", due to the rich, dark soil that was produced by the annual flooding of the Nile River. This fertile soil was essential for the country's agriculture and allowed the Egyptians to cultivate crops, making it the basis of their economy and livelihood. The name Kemet was a reflection of the land's unique geography and the importance of the Nile's floods, which brought nutrient-rich silt that made the land suitable for farming. A business model refers to the underlying framework that describes how a company creates, delivers, and captures value from its products or services.
